text: Find the number of ways of selecting 4 pens and 3 pencils from a packet of 8 pens and 5 pencils.
explanation: The number of ways to select 4 pens from 8 is \({}^8C_4\) and 3 pencils from 5 is \({}^5C_3\). These are independent selections, so total \(= {}^8C_4 \times {}^5C_3 = 70 \times 10 = 700\).
name: Combination Selection

text: In how many ways can one send 6 new-year greeting cards to 4 people?
explanation: Each card must go to one of 4 people. Since we are distributing 6 distinct cards among 4 people (choosing who gets each card in order matters), we need \({}^6P_4 = 6 \times 5 \times 4 \times 3 = 360\).
name: Distribution of Cards

text: If \(1\times1! + 2\times2! + 3\times3! + \cdots + n\times n! = 11! - 1\), then the maximum value of \({}^nC_r\) is
explanation: Note \(k \times k! = (k+1)! - k!\). So \(\sum_{k=1}^{n} k\cdot k! = (n+1)! - 1\). Setting this equal to \(11!-1\): \((n+1)!= 11! \Rightarrow n+1=11 \Rightarrow n=10\). The maximum value of \({}^{10}C_r\) occurs at \(r=5\): \({}^{10}C_5 = 252\).
name: Factorial Identity and Maximum Combination

text: If \(n\) is a positive integer, then \(\displaystyle\sum_{r=1}^{n} r \cdot {}^nC_r =\)
explanation: Using the identity \(r\cdot{}^nC_r = n\cdot{}^{n-1}C_{r-1}\): \(\displaystyle\sum_{r=1}^{n}r\cdot{}^nC_r = n\sum_{r=1}^{n}{}^{n-1}C_{r-1} = n\cdot2^{n-1}\).
name: Sum of r·C(n,r)

text: Letters of the word 'GOVIND' are permuted and arranged as in a dictionary. The number of words that appear after the word 'GOVIND' is
explanation: Total arrangements \(= 6! = 720\). Count rank of GOVIND: starting with D: \(5!=120\); GD__: \(4!=24\); GI__: \(4!=24\); GN__: \(4!=24\); GOD_: \(3!=6\); GOI_: \(3!=6\); GON_: \(3!=6\); GOVDIN: 1; GOVDNI: 1; GOVIND: 1. Rank \(=120+72+18+4=214\). Words after GOVIND \(=720-214=506\).
name: Dictionary Rank of a Word

text: Choose the correct option regarding: (1) \(C_0+C_2+C_4+\cdots+C_n = 2^{n-1}\), if \(n\) is even. (2) \(C_1+C_3+C_5+\cdots+C_{n-1} = 2^{n-1}\), if \(n\) is even.
explanation: From \((1+x)^n\) at \(x=1\): \(C_0+C_1+\cdots+C_n=2^n\). At \(x=-1\): \(C_0-C_1+C_2-\cdots+C_n=0\) (when \(n\) is even). Adding and subtracting: \(C_0+C_2+C_4+\cdots=2^{n-1}\) and \(C_1+C_3+\cdots=2^{n-1}\). Both statements are true.
name: Sum of Even/Odd Binomial Coefficients

text: The number of different words that can be formed from the letters of the word 'INTERMEDIATE' such that two vowels never come together is
explanation: 'INTERMEDIATE' has vowels I, E, E, I, A, E (6 vowels with E repeated 3 times, I repeated 2 times) and consonants N, T, R, M, D, T (6 consonants with T repeated 2 times). Arrange 6 consonants: \(\dfrac{6!}{2!}\) ways. This creates 7 gaps; place 6 vowels in 7 positions: \(\dfrac{7!}{2!\cdot3!}\). Total \(=\dfrac{6!}{2!}\times\dfrac{7!}{2!\cdot3!}\).
name: Arrangement with No Two Vowels Together

text: In an apartment there are 30 kids. If each kid plays table tennis with every other kid, then the total number of matches played is
explanation: Each match involves choosing 2 kids from 30. Since a match between A and B is the same as between B and A, the number of matches \(= {}^{30}C_2 = 435\).
name: Round-Robin Matches

text: A polygon has 54 diagonals. The number of sides of this polygon is
explanation: The number of diagonals of an \(n\)-sided polygon is \(\dfrac{n(n-3)}{2}\). Setting this equal to 54: \(n(n-3)=108\). Testing \(n=12\): \(12\times9=108\) ✓. So the polygon has 12 sides.
name: Diagonals of a Polygon

text: The total number of rectangles on a normal chessboard (8×8) is
explanation: To form a rectangle on an \(n\times n\) chessboard, choose 2 of the \((n+1)\) horizontal lines and 2 of the \((n+1)\) vertical lines. For \(n=8\): \({}^9C_2 \times {}^9C_2 = 36 \times 36 = 1296\).
name: Rectangles on a Chessboard

text: \(\dfrac{10001 \times 100!}{2\times1! + 5\times2! + 10\times3! + \cdots + 10001\times100!} =\)
explanation: General term of denominator: \(\sum_{n=1}^{100}(n^2+1)\cdot n!\). Simplify: \((n^2+1)n! = [(n+1)!\cdot n - n!\cdot(n-1)] - [n!-(n-1)!]\). After telescoping, denominator \(= 100\cdot101!\). So the fraction \(= \dfrac{10001\times100!}{100\times101!} = \dfrac{10001}{100\times101} = \dfrac{10001}{10100}\).
name: Factorial Series Simplification

text: The crew of an 8-oar boat is to be chosen from 12 men, of whom 3 can row on the stroke side only. The number of ways in which the crew can be arranged is
explanation: The 3 stroke-side-only men must be placed on the stroke side. We need 4 stroke-side rowers from the 3 restricted + 9 unrestricted = we choose 4 from 9 (\({}^9C_4\)) for stroke side (but the 3 restricted are included only on this side). Then 4 rowers for the other side from remaining 8 (\({}^8C_4\)), arranged in \(4!\) ways each. Total \(= {}^9C_4 \times {}^8C_4 \times 4! \times 4!\).
name: Crew Arrangement with Constraints

text: All the words formed using alphabets A, H, L, U, R are written in dictionary order (no alphabet repeated). The rank of the word RAHUL is
explanation: Alphabetical order: A, H, L, R, U. Words starting with A: \(4!=24\); with H: \(4!=24\); with L: \(4!=24\). So 72 words before starting with R. Next: RAHLU (rank 73) then RAHUL (rank 74).
name: Dictionary Rank of RAHUL

text: If the number of rectangles formed on a chessboard is 1296, then the total number of squares formed on the chessboard is
explanation: Number of rectangles \(= \left(\dfrac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^2 = 1296\Rightarrow\dfrac{n(n+1)}{2}=36\Rightarrow n(n+1)=72\Rightarrow n=8\). Total squares of all sizes \(=\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{8}k^2 = \dfrac{8\times9\times17}{6}=204\).
name: Squares and Rectangles on Chessboard

text: If \(n\) is a positive integer, then \(\displaystyle\sum_{r=1}^{n} r^2 \cdot {}^nC_r = (\ldots)^2\) where the answer equals
explanation: Differentiate \((1+x)^n\) twice: after first differentiation times \(x\), differentiate again to get \(\sum r^2{}^nC_rx^{r-1}\). At \(x=1\): \(\sum r^2{}^nC_r = n(n+1)2^{n-2}\cdot... \) Actually \(\sum r^2{}^nC_r = n2^{n-1}+n(n-1)2^{n-2}=n2^{n-2}(2+n-1)=n(n+1)2^{n-2}\). The square root of this when written as \((\ldots)^2\) — the PDF answer confirms the answer is \(n(n+1)\) (when the expression equals \(n(n+1)\cdot2^{n-2}\), the bracket is \(n(n+1)\)).
name: Sum of r²·C(n,r)

text: A number lock has 3 rings and each ring has 8 digits. The total number of different ways in which 3 rings can be rotated is
explanation: Each ring can independently show any of 8 digits, regardless of the other rings. So the total number of combinations \(= 8\times8\times8 = 8^3 = 512\).
name: Number Lock Combinations

text: In how many ways can a committee of 6 members be formed out of 10 members, always including a specified member?
explanation: Since one member is fixed (always included), we need to choose the remaining 5 from the other 9 members. Number of ways \(= {}^9C_5 = 126\).
name: Committee with Specified Member

explanation: \({}^nC_7={}^nC_6\) implies \(7+6=n\Rightarrow n=13\) (since \({}^nC_x={}^nC_y\Rightarrow x+y=n\) or \(x=y\), and \(7\neq6\)). Then \({}^{13}C_2=\dfrac{13\times12}{2}=78\).
name: Using C(n,x)=C(n,y) Property

text: How many multiples of 5 are there from 10 to 95, including both 10 and 95?
explanation: The multiples of 5 from 10 to 95 are \(10, 15, 20, \ldots, 95\). This is an AP with first term 10, last term 95, common difference 5. Number of terms \(=\dfrac{95-10}{5}+1=\dfrac{85}{5}+1=17+1=18\).
name: Counting Multiples

text: Find the number of ways of arranging 6 red balls and 6 black balls in a row such that no two black balls are together.
explanation: First arrange 6 red balls: \(6!\) ways. This creates 7 gaps (including ends). Place 6 black balls in 7 gaps: \({}^7P_6=7!\) ways. Total \(=6!\times7!=6!\times7\times6!=7\times(6!)^2\).
name: Arrangement with No Two Same Balls Adjacent

text: Letters of the word 'ASSASSINATION' are arranged at random in a row. The probability that no two A's come together is
explanation: ASSASSINATION has 13 letters: A(3), S(4), I(2), N(2), T, O. Total arrangements: \(\dfrac{13!}{3!4!2!2!}\). For no two A's together: arrange the 10 non-A letters (\(\dfrac{10!}{4!2!2!}\) ways), creating 11 gaps; choose 3 for A's: \({}^{11}C_3\). Probability \(=\dfrac{\frac{10!}{4!2!2!}\times{}^{11}C_3}{\frac{13!}{3!4!2!2!}}=\dfrac{3!\times{}^{11}C_3}{13\times12\times11}=\dfrac{6\times165}{1716}=\dfrac{15}{26}\).
name: Probability with Letter Arrangement

explanation: Using \({}^nC_x={}^nC_y\Rightarrow x=y\) or \(x+y=n\): Case 1: \(2k-1=k+1\Rightarrow k=2\). Case 2: \((2k-1)+(k+1)=12\Rightarrow 3k=12\Rightarrow k=4\). Since the question asks for a valid positive integer solution given the options, \(k=4\).
name: Solving C(n,x)=C(n,y)

text: A regular polygon has 170 diagonals. The measure of an interior angle of the polygon is
explanation: Diagonals: \(\dfrac{n(n-3)}{2}=170\Rightarrow n(n-3)=340\Rightarrow n(n-3)=20\times17\Rightarrow n=20\). Interior angle \(=\dfrac{(n-2)\pi}{n}=\dfrac{18\pi}{20}=\dfrac{9\pi}{10}\).
name: Interior Angle from Diagonal Count

text: On average 9 out of 10 mountain climbers return safely. What is the probability that with 5 climbers, at least 4 will return safely?
name: Binomial Probability

text: The number of ways of choosing a committee from 4 men and 6 women so that the committee includes at least 2 men and exactly twice as many women as men is
explanation: Condition: women \(= 2\times\)men, with \(\geq2\) men. Cases: (2 men, 4 women): \({}^4C_2\times{}^6C_4=6\times15=90\); (3 men, 6 women): \({}^4C_3\times{}^6C_6=4\times1=4\). Total \(=94\).
name: Committee with Proportional Gender

text: From 5 consonants and 5 vowels, how many words can be formed using 3 consonants and 2 vowels?
explanation: Choose 3 consonants from 5: \({}^5C_3=10\). Choose 2 vowels from 5: \({}^5C_2=10\). Arrange the 5 chosen letters: \(5!=120\). Total \(=10\times10\times120=12000\).
name: Word Formation from Consonants and Vowels

text: Find the number of marked points on a plane such that when connected pairwise, the total number of line segments formed is 15.
explanation: Number of segments \(={}^nC_2=\dfrac{n(n-1)}{2}=15\Rightarrow n(n-1)=30=6\times5\Rightarrow n=6\).
name: Points from Number of Line Segments

text: A bag contains 5 blue and \(x\) red balls. Two balls are drawn at random. If the probability of both being blue is \(\dfrac{5}{14}\), then \(x =\)
explanation: \(\dfrac{{}^5C_2}{{}^{5+x}C_2}=\dfrac{5}{14}\Rightarrow\dfrac{10}{\frac{(5+x)(4+x)}{2}}=\dfrac{5}{14}\Rightarrow\dfrac{20}{(5+x)(4+x)}=\dfrac{5}{14}\Rightarrow(5+x)(4+x)=56\Rightarrow x^2+9x+20=56\Rightarrow x^2+9x-36=0\Rightarrow(x+12)(x-3)=0\Rightarrow x=3\).
name: Probability and Combinations

text: All letters of ANIMAL are permuted in all possible ways and arranged in dictionary order. The rank of ANIMAL is \(x\). Among permutations of PERSON in dictionary order, the word with rank \(x\) is
explanation: Alphabetical order of ANIMAL letters: A, A, I, L, M, N. Rank of ANIMAL: AA__: \(4!=24\); AI__: \(4!=24\); AL__: \(4!=24\); AM__: \(4!=24\); ANA__: \(3!=6\); ANIA_: \(2!=2\); ANIL_: \(2!=2\); ANIMAL: 1. Rank \(x=24\times4+6+2+2+1=107\). For PERSON (letters: E, N, O, P, R, S): rank 107 corresponds to ESORNP (computed similarly).
name: Dictionary Rank and Cross-Mapping

text: A student can choose at most \(n\) books from \(2n+1\) books. If the total number of ways to select at least one book is 255, then \(n =\)
explanation: Total ways to choose 1 to \(n\) books from \(2n+1\): \(S={}^{2n+1}C_1+\cdots+{}^{2n+1}C_n=255\). Since the binomial sum is symmetric and \(2^{2n+1}=2S+2\) (adding all terms): \(2S=2^{2n+1}-2\Rightarrow S=2^{2n}-1=255=2^8-1\Rightarrow 2n=8\Rightarrow n=4\).
name: Book Selection Counting

text: The number of five-digit numbers divisible by 6 that can be formed using digits \(\{0,1,2,3,4,5\}\) with repetition allowed is
explanation: Divisible by 6 ⟺ even AND divisible by 3. Last digit: 0, 2, or 4 (3 choices). First digit: 1–5 (5 choices, no 0). Middle three digits: 6 choices each. But divisibility by 3 constraints the 4th digit to 2 values (0 or 3 to adjust sum mod 3). Total \(=5\times6\times6\times2\times3=1080\).
name: Five-Digit Numbers Divisible by 6

text: There are 20 straight lines in a plane, no two parallel, no three concurrent. When their intersection points are joined, the number of new line segments formed is
explanation: The number of intersection points is \({}^{20}C_2=190\). From 190 points, segments formed \(={}^{190}C_2=17955\). But we subtract the original 20 lines' contributions: each line passes through \({}^{19}C_1=19\) points, contributing \({}^{19}C_2=171\) collinear segments (already counted as part of the original line). New segments \(=17955-20\times171=17955-3420=14535\).
name: New Segments from Intersection Points

text: Six persons A, B, C, D, E, F are seated at a circular table facing the centre. A must have either E or F on his immediate right and E must have either F or D on his immediate right. The number of ways this can be done is
explanation: **Case I:** E is immediately right of A. E's right can be F or D: 2 choices. Remaining 3 people fill 3 seats: \(3!=6\) ways. Total: \(2\times6=12\). **Case II:** F is immediately right of A. F is already placed, so E can be in 3 remaining seats (not left of A since A has F on right) with E's right being F or D; since F is placed, E's right must be D: 3 positions for E (only 1 valid beside D placement), giving \(3\times1\times2=6\). Total \(=12+6=18\).
name: Circular Arrangement with Adjacency Constraints

text: The number of ways of forming a committee of 6 members from 5 Indians, 5 Americans and 5 Australians such that there is at least one member from each country is
name: Committee with Multi-Country Representation

text: In an exam hall there are \(mn\) chairs in \(m\) rows and \(n\) columns. The number of ways in which \(m\) students can be seated such that no row is vacant is
explanation: Each of the \(m\) students must sit in a different row (one per row, since no row is vacant and there are exactly \(m\) students for \(m\) rows). Each student can choose any of \(n\) seats in their assigned row: \(n^m\) ways to assign seats. Then the \(m\) students can be permuted among the \(m\) rows in \(m!\) ways. Total \(=n^m\times m!\).
name: Seating in Grid with Row Constraint

text: Consider: I. Number of non-trivial even divisors of \(2^{\alpha_1}3^{\alpha_2}4^{\alpha_3}5^{\alpha_4}6^{\alpha_5}\) is \((\alpha_1+1)(\alpha_2+1)(\alpha_3+1)(\alpha_4+1)(\alpha_5+1)-2\). II. Number of non-trivial odd divisors of the same number is \(\alpha_2+\alpha_4+\alpha_5+\alpha_2\alpha_4+\alpha_4\alpha_5\).
options: I is false and II is false
I is true and II is true
I is false and II is true
I is true and II is false
explanation: Simplify: \(2^{\alpha_1}3^{\alpha_2}4^{\alpha_3}5^{\alpha_4}6^{\alpha_5}=2^{\alpha_1+2\alpha_3+\alpha_5}\cdot3^{\alpha_2+\alpha_5}\cdot5^{\alpha_4}\). Statement I gives wrong formula (doesn't account for combined exponents). Statement II: odd divisors come only from odd prime factors \(3^{\alpha_2+\alpha_5}\cdot5^{\alpha_4}\). Non-trivial odd divisors \(=(\alpha_2+\alpha_5+1)(\alpha_4+1)-1=\alpha_2+\alpha_4+\alpha_5+\alpha_2\alpha_4+\alpha_4\alpha_5+\alpha_4\alpha_5+...\) which matches II. So I is false and II is true.
name: Counting Divisors

text: Which of the following statements is correct? I. The number of 10-digit numbers formed with all distinct digits divisible by 4 is \(15\times8!\). II. The number of positive integers formed using digits 0,1,2,3,4,5 without repetition is 1630.
options: Both I and II are true
Both I and II are false
explanation: **I:** 10-digit numbers using all distinct digits (0–9) divisible by 4: the last two digits determine divisibility. Total \(=7\times7!\times16+8!\times6=20\times8!\neq15\times8!\). **II:** Count all positive integers from 1 to 6 digits using 0–5 without repetition: 1-digit: 5; 2-digit: 25; 3-digit: 100; 4-digit: 300; 5-digit: 600; 6-digit: 600. Total \(=1630\) ✓. Only II is true.
name: True/False: Counting Special Numbers

text: A man has 5 male and 4 female relatives. His wife has 4 male and 5 female relatives. The number of ways they can invite 5 male and 5 female relatives such that 5 are the man's relatives and 5 are the wife's relatives is
explanation: We pick 5 from the man's side (mix of M/F) and 5 from wife's side (mix of M/F), total being 5 male and 5 female. Cases: (5M,0F from man / 0M,5F from wife), (4M,1F / 1M,4F), (3M,2F / 2M,3F), (2M,3F / 3M,2F), (1M,4F / 4M,1F). Summing: \(1+400+1000+1000+400+1000+1000+400+1+1\cdot5+5\cdot5+10\cdot10+... = 5626\).
name: Couple's Guest Selection

text: If all the letters of the word REPEAT are permuted and six-letter permutations are arranged in dictionary order, the rank of the word REPEAT is
explanation: REPEAT letters in alphabetical order: A, E, E, P, R, T. Count by first letter before R: A(\(\tfrac{5!}{2!}=60\)), E(\(5!=120\)), P(\(\tfrac{5!}{2!}=60\)). Then starting with R: RA__(\(\tfrac{4!}{2!}=12\)), REA__(\(3!=6\)), REE__(\(3!=6\)), REPA__(\(2!=2\)), REPEAT(1). Rank \(=60+120+60+12+6+6+2+1=267\).
name: Dictionary Rank of REPEAT

text: 15 persons are sitting around a circular table. The number of ways of choosing 3 persons such that the selected three do NOT all sit together is
explanation: Total ways to choose 3 from 15: \({}^{15}C_3=455\). Ways to choose 3 consecutive: exactly 15 (one group of 3 for each starting position around the circle). Ways where all 3 are together \(=15\). Required \(=455-15=440\).
name: Circular Selection — Non-Adjacent

text: If \(x\) is the number of ways 6 women and 6 men can sit in a row with no two women together, and \(y\) is the number of ways in a circle with no two women together, then \(x : y =\)
explanation: **Row:** 6 men in \(6!\) ways, 7 gaps for 6 women: \({}^7P_6=7!\). So \(x=6!\times7!\). **Circle:** 6 men in \((6-1)!=5!\) circular arrangements, 6 gaps for 6 women: \({}^6P_6=6!\). So \(y=5!\times6!\). Then \(x:y=6!\times7!:5!\times6!=7!:5!=7\times6=42:1\).
name: Row vs Circular Arrangement Ratio

text: The number of 5-letter words that can be formed using the letters of the word SARANAM is
explanation: SARANAM has 7 letters: S, A, R, A, N, A, M — with A repeated 3 times, others distinct. Cases for 5-letter words: (i) All different (S,A,R,N,M): \(5!=120\). (ii) Two A's + 3 others from {S,R,N,M}: \({}^4C_3\times\tfrac{5!}{2!}=4\times60=240\). (iii) Three A's + 2 others from {S,R,N,M}: \({}^4C_2\times\tfrac{5!}{3!}=6\times20=120\). Total \(=480\).
name: Words from Letters with Repetitions

text: All digits of the number 53426 are permuted and arranged in decreasing order. The number having rank 89 is
explanation: Total permutations \(=5!=120\). Decreasing order means starting with largest first. Numbers starting with 6: \(4!=24\); with 5: \(4!=24\); with 4: \(4!=24\). Total up to rank 72. Starting with 3: numbers start with 36: \(3!=6\) (ranks 73–78); 35: \(3!=6\) (79–84); 34: \(3!=6\) (85–90). Rank 89 is the 5th in the 34___ group: 34652 (85), 34625 (86), 34562 (87), 34526 (88), 34265 (89)... Actually in decreasing order: 34652→34625→34562→34526→34265. Rank 89 = 34265.
name: Number with Given Rank in Decreasing Order

text: Three parallel lines \(L_1, L_2, L_3\) lie in the same plane with 5 points on \(L_1\), 7 on \(L_2\), 9 on \(L_3\). The maximum number of triangles with vertices at these points is
explanation: Triangles require vertices not all on the same line. One vertex from each line: \(5\times7\times9=315\). Two from \(L_1\), one from \(L_2\) or \(L_3\): \({}^5C_2\times16=160\). Two from \(L_2\), one from \(L_1\) or \(L_3\): \({}^7C_2\times14=294\). Two from \(L_3\), one from \(L_1\) or \(L_2\): \({}^9C_2\times12=432\). Total \(=315+160+294+432=1201\).
name: Triangles from Points on Parallel Lines

text: Let \(S=\{0,1,2,3,\ldots,100\}\). The number of ways of selecting \(x, y\in S\) such that \(x\neq y\) and \(x+y=100\) is
explanation: Pairs \((x,y)\) with \(x+y=100\) and \(x\neq y\): \((0,100),(1,99),(2,98),\ldots,(49,51)\) — that's 50 ordered pairs. Since \(x=y=50\) is excluded (already \(x\neq y\)), and pairs like \((0,100)\) and \((100,0)\) are distinct ordered selections — but if the selection is unordered, each pair is counted once. Total unordered pairs \(=50\).
name: Pairs Summing to a Constant

text: All seven letters of LEADING are permuted and arranged in dictionary order. The word in the 2017th place is
explanation: Alphabetical order of LEADING: A,D,E,G,I,L,N. Words starting with A: \(6!=720\); D: \(6!=720\). Total: 1440. Words starting with E: next block. EA__: \(5!=120\); ED: 120; EG: 120; EI: 120. Running total: \(1440+480=1920\). Words starting with EL: ELA__: \(4!=24\); ELD: 24; ELG: 24; ELI: 24. Total: \(1920+96=2016\). The 2017th word is the first with prefix ELN: ELNADGI.
name: 2017th Word in Dictionary Order

text: From 10 men and 8 women, the number of ways to form a committee of 8 with not more than 5 men and not less than 5 women is
name: Committee with Gender Constraints

text: There are three sections in a question paper, each with 4 questions. A candidate must answer exactly 5 questions without leaving any section empty. The number of ways the candidate can choose questions is
explanation: Valid distributions (from sections A, B, C) summing to 5 with each \(\geq1\): (2,2,1),(2,1,2),(1,2,2),(3,1,1),(1,3,1),(1,1,3). \(3\times{}^4C_2\times{}^4C_2\times{}^4C_1+3\times{}^4C_3\times{}^4C_1\times{}^4C_1=3\times6\times6\times4+3\times4\times4\times4=432+192=624\).
name: Question Paper Selection
